latest.title: Engineering Agreement with Cactus Wellhead LLC
latest.content:
20 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8941M
Plexus Holdings Plc
20 July 2026
This announcement contains inside information
20th July 2026
Plexus Holdings PLC
("Plexus" or the "Company")
Engineering Agreement with Cactus Wellhead LLC
Plexus Holdings PLC (AIM: POS),
the AIM quoted wellhead services business
, is pleased to announce that it has entered into an agreement with Cactus Wellhead LLC ("Cactus"), an international service company, to develop and qualify a range of wellhead products for offshore energy applications.
HIGHLIGHTS
·
Two-year engineering programme to design and test new products for Cactus
·
Initial commitment payment of £1m, secured by a debenture
·
Additional revenue of up to approx. £1.5m over the course of the programme
·
Work to commence immediately
Plexus' CEO Craig Hendrie said:
"This work has been awarded to Plexus as a result of our reputation for technology excellence and successful product development and qualification testing over many decades.
"We are very excited and proud to be given the opportunity to take on this work, and I commend the team at Plexus for their dedication and professionalism, which has been instrumental in securing this opportunity.
"Using Plexus' existing engineering capability and testing infrastructure for an international company gives us the opportunity to continue to retain and build our capabilities in Aberdeen at a time when investment in the North Sea has been under pressure."
DETAILS
Under the terms of the agreement, Plexus will undertake engineering, analysis and testing services for Cactus, leveraging its core expertise in wellhead engineering and offshore energy applications.
The Company will receive an initial commitment payment of £1m and expects to generate additional revenue of approximately £1.5m over the duration of the programme, depending on the customer requirements. There is also potential for the scope of work to expand during the programme, which may result in additional revenue.
All work will be carried out at Plexus' operational facility in Aberdeen by its highly skilled and experienced engineers and technicians.
The initial £1m prepayment will be secured by a debenture over all Company assets, until the work under the contract has been discharged, which is expected to occur within the 2-year contract period.
ENDS
For further information please visit
www.plexusplc.com
or contact:
Plexus Holdings PLC
Craig Hendrie, CEO
Mike Park, CFO
Tel: 01224 774222
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
Derrick Lee / Pearl Kellie
Tel: 0131 220 6939
St Brides Partners Ltd
Isabel de Salis / Ana
Ribeiro
plexus@stbridespartners.co.uk
NOTES
Plexus Holdings plc (AIM: POS) is an IP-led company specialising in developing and providing wellhead systems and associated products and services for offshore operations.
Headquartered in Aberdeen, the Company focuses on providing wellheads for Jack-up rig operations such as exploration and appraisal drilling, Plug & Abandonment work and emerging markets for Carbon Capture & Storage, hydrogen production and storage and geothermal. Plexus has a collaboration agreement with another industry player to supply Exact adjustable wellhead systems for Jack-up applications and supports these operations with a range of proprietary equipment and tools, and project engineering services.
Plexus is known for its highly innovative POS-GRIP® wellhead technology and HG® metal-to-metal sealing systems, for which licences have been sold to other industry players. Plexus continues to develop products based on POS-GRIP technology for challenging surface production wellhead platforms, special projects and connector and subsea applications such as the Python® subsea wellhead system.
Plexus has always been at the forefront of innovation to reduce risk and improve performance in the energy industry, such as developing through-BOP wellhead systems which help to prevent blow-outs, and POS-GRIP "HG" Seals which are leak-free throughout field life and so reduce methane emissions and minimise maintenance costs. These innovations support the oil and gas industry's ESG and NetZero, and in recognition of this, Plexus was Awarded the London Stock Exchange's Green Economy Mark in 2021.
For more information visit:
https://www.plexusplc.com/

company_30s.what_they_do: POS.L supplies wellhead equipment and services to the oil and gas industry, installing and maintaining the critical pressure‑control hardware that sits at the top of a wellbore.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company operates in a niche segment of the energy services sector, where the demand for reliable wellhead solutions is tied directly to drilling activity and new exploration projects. Its equipment is required for every well, giving it a steady, long‑term revenue stream if it can keep up with industry growth.
company_30s.current_state: POS.L is currently in a rebuilding phase. Recent filings show it secured a loan facility and entered a £1.5 m framework agreement with a new client, suggesting it is actively pursuing contracts to offset a period of cash burn. The share price is trading at a very low level relative to its historic valuation, indicating a steep discount to any perceived intrinsic value.

rns_narrative: The company has shifted focus from holding to executing new wellhead contracts, evidenced by the engineering agreement with Cactus Wellhead LLC and the £1.5 m framework agreement. This indicates a move from a passive position to active revenue generation.
Financial updates – interim results, AGM statements, and a loan facility – show that POS.L is tightening its balance sheet and securing liquidity to weather the next few quarters.
Collectively, these announcements suggest the firm is stabilising its operations and preparing for a modest uptick in cash flow once new contracts come online.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent skepticism – the community is largely quiet, with only a handful of neutral comments and no clear rallying cry.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There are no active bullish voices; the lack of chatter is a sign that the market has not yet recognised the potential upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the company’s thin cash position and the fact that its recent contracts are still in the early engineering phase, meaning revenue may not materialise for months.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has not shifted – it remains flat and muted, offering a blank canvas for a contrarian play.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead, meaning any forthcoming positive news has the potential to surprise the market dramatically.

near_term_catalysts: Finalisation of the engineering agreement with Cactus Wellhead LLC – a signed, deliverable contract would be a clear sign that revenue is on the horizon.
Completion of the £1.5 m framework agreement and the first payment tranche, which would immediately improve cash flow.
Any announcement of drilling activity in the regions where the new wellheads are being deployed, as this would directly drive demand for the company’s services.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a spike in trading volume that coincides with contract signing, a sudden jump in the 52‑week high, or a clear statement from management about expected revenue from the new deals.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle – it has fallen far below its recent highs and is still trading near the bottom of a deep trough. The APEX score suggests a tight range, so a breakout above the upper support would be a strong confirmation of mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The signal colour is GREEN (deep crash bottom). Historically GREEN signals have an average return of ~39% with a 40% win rate. If the stock recovers to just 50% of its previous high, the upside is roughly double the current price, offering a 100% gain for a 70% downside risk that has already been taken on by the market.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the new contracts fail to materialise or that the company’s cash burn continues, forcing it to delay or cancel the agreements. In that scenario the stock could slide back down to its current level or below, wiping out the upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 70% of the investment (already absorbed by the market). Potential gain: 100% if the share rebounds to the midpoint of its 52‑week range. The risk‑reward ratio is roughly 1:1.4, which is attractive for a contrarian play that already bears the downside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear volume spike that confirms the contract signing, sizing the position so that a 70% loss would be the maximum you are willing to accept. Place a stop just below the recent low to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for the first payment under the £1.5 m framework agreement and any press release confirming the start of the engineering work. A 10% jump in price on volume above 1.5× the 20‑day average would be a good entry trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a failure to secure the new contracts or a significant downgrade in its liquidity position, consider exiting or reducing exposure.

ai_insights.bottom_line: POS.L sits at the bottom of a deep crash, has just secured new wellhead contracts that could unlock cash flow, and enjoys a dead market sentiment – a classic contrarian setup that offers a 1:1.4 risk‑reward profile for those willing to stomach the downside.
